privacyPolicy.metaDescription: How WatashiWa handles your data. Transparent, secure, and yours to control.
privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy

section1.title: Our Commitment
section1.content: Your data belongs to you. We collect only what's necessary to deliver personalized learning. This policy explains what we gather, why, and how you control it.

section2.title: What We Collect
section2.content: We gather only what's needed to personalize your learning:
section2.item1: Account: Email, name, profile picture (via OAuth if provided)
section2.item2: Study Data: Your cards, decks, review history, SRS states, and progress
section2.item3: Your Content: Decks you create, community tips, and contributions
section2.item4: Usage: Anonymous analytics via PostHog to improve the experience
section2.item5: Technical: Device type, browser, IP (for security and performance)

section3.title: How We Use It
section3.item1: Personalization: SRS calculations, intervention triggers, and adaptive card variants
section3.item2: Improvement: Analyzing patterns to fix issues and enhance features
section3.item3: Communication: Account updates, service changes, and important notices
section3.item4: Security: Preventing abuse and protecting your account

section4.title: Security
section4.item1: PostgreSQL databases (encrypted at rest) managed via Prisma
section4.item4: Supabase: Authentication and database (GDPR compliant, SOC 2 certified)

section5.title: Third-Party Services
section5.item1Desc: Authentication only. Google's privacy policy applies.
section5.item2Desc: Anonymous analytics to improve the app. GDPR compliant.
section5.item3Desc: Authentication and database. GDPR compliant, SOC 2 certified.

section6.title: Your Rights
section6.item1: Access: Request a copy of all your data
section6.item3: Deletion: Remove your account and all associated data
section6.item4: Export: Download your data in JSON format anytime

section7.title: Children's Privacy
section7.content: Our service is not intended for children under 13 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

termsOfService.metaDescription: Terms and conditions for using WatashiWa. Simple, clear, fair.
termsOfService.title: Terms of Service

section1.title: Agreement
section1.content: By using WatashiWa, you agree to these terms. If you don't agree, don't use the service.

section2.title: Your Responsibilities
section2.item3: Providing accurate information when signing up
section2.item4: Notifying us immediately of unauthorized access

section3.title: Your Content
section3.content: You own what you create—decks, tips, comments. By sharing, you grant us a license to display and distribute it within WatashiWa.
section3.content2: You must ensure your content is legal and doesn't infringe others' rights.

section4.title: Intellectual Property
section4.content: WatashiWa's code, design, and features are protected by copyright. However, we're open source—our codebase is available on GitHub under an open-source license.
section4.content2: See our GitHub repository for licensing details.

section5.title: Prohibited Uses
section5.item1: Break laws or encourage illegal activity

section6.title: Service Availability
section6.content: We aim for reliability but can't guarantee 100% uptime. Service may be unavailable during maintenance or due to circumstances beyond our control.

section7.title: Limitation of Liability
section7.content: WatashiWa is provided "as is" without warranties. We're not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages from your use of the service.

section8.title: Termination
section8.content: We may terminate or suspend your account immediately, without notice, for violations of these terms.

section9.title: Changes
section9.content: We may modify these terms at any time. We'll notify you of material changes via email or in-app.

cookiePolicy.metaDescription: How WatashiWa uses cookies. Simple, transparent, under your control.
cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy

section1.title: What Are Cookies
section1.content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device. They help websites remember your preferences and improve your experience.

section2.title: What We Use
section2.desc1: Required for the app to work. Handle authentication, security, and sessions.
section2.desc2: PostHog cookies collect anonymous usage data to help us improve the app and fix issues.
section2.desc3: Remember your language, theme, and other settings for a personalized experience.

section3.title: Control
section3.item1: Most browsers let you accept or refuse cookies
section3.item2: You can delete cookies already set on your device
section3.item3: Disabling cookies may affect app functionality

section4.title: Third-Party Cookies
section4.content: PostHog (analytics) may set cookies. Their privacy policy applies. Disable cookies in your browser to opt out.

section5.title: Browser Settings
section5.content: To manage cookies in your browser:
section5.item1: Chrome: Settings > Privacy and Security > Cookies and other site data
section5.item2: Firefox: Options > Privacy & Security > Cookies and Site Data
section5.item3: Safari: Preferences > Privacy > Cookies and website data
section5.item4: Edge: Settings > Privacy, search, and services > Cookies and site permissions

dataRights.metaDescription: Your data, your control. Access, export, or delete anytime. GDPR compliant.
dataRights.title: Your Data Rights

section1.title: Your Rights
section1.content: Under GDPR and data protection laws, you have:
section1.item1Desc: Request a copy of all your personal data.
section1.item2Desc: Request fixes to inaccurate or incomplete data.
section1.item3Desc: Request removal of your account and all associated data.
section1.item4Desc: Export your data in JSON format anytime from settings.
section1.item5Desc: Request limits on how we process your data.
section1.item6Desc: Object to certain processing, such as analytics.

section2.title: How to Exercise
section2.content: Contact us with your request. We respond within 30 days.

section3.title: Export Your Data
section3.content: Export directly from settings. Your download includes:
section3.content2: All flashcards, decks, study progress, review history, and account info in JSON format.

section4.title: Delete Account
section4.step3: Confirm deletion. This cannot be undone.
section4.warning: Warning: This permanently removes all study data, decks, and progress. Irreversible.

Contact.metaDescription: Reach out for support, feature requests, or to report issues. We respond within 48 hours.
Contact.title: Contact
Contact.subtitle: Questions? Issues? Ideas? We're here.

section2.title: What We Handle
section2.item3Desc: Suggestions for improvements or new capabilities
section2.item4Desc: How to use features, best practices, or methodology

section3.title: Response Time
section3.content: We aim to respond within 48 hours. For urgent issues, create a GitHub issue labeled 'urgent'.

section4.title: Community
section4.item1: GitHub Issues - Report bugs, request features
section4.item2: GitHub Discussions - Share ideas, ask questions

Help.metaDescription: Get help with WatashiWa. Find answers to common questions about getting started, flashcards, account management, and troubleshooting.
Help.title: Help Center
Help.subtitle: Find answers to common questions and learn how to get the most out of WatashiWa.

q1.question: How do I start learning?
q1.answer: After creating your account, complete your profile setup to personalize your experience. Then, browse the deck library to find decks that interest you, or create your own custom deck. Set your daily learning goals and start reviewing cards at your own pace.

q2.question: What is the CUBE method?
q2.answer: The CUBE method (Context, Understanding, Blocking, Encoding) is WatashiWa's approach to vocabulary learning. It uses semantic connections, spaced repetition, and interference shielding to help words stick naturally in your memory.

q3.question: How do I set up my profile?
q3.answer: Go to your profile settings (click the settings icon in the navigation bar) to set your language preference, timezone, daily goals, and other learning preferences. Your profile setup helps personalize your learning experience.

q1.question: How does spaced repetition work?
q1.answer: WatashiWa uses an intelligent spaced repetition system (SRS) that adapts to your memory patterns. Cards you find difficult appear more frequently, while mastered cards appear less often. This helps optimize your learning efficiency.

q2.question: What are the rating options?
q2.answer: When reviewing cards, you can rate your recall: 'Again' (forgot), 'Hard' (struggled), 'Good' (remembered), or 'Easy' (knew instantly). These ratings help the system schedule the next review at the optimal time.

q3.question: Can I create my own decks?
q3.answer: Yes! You can create custom decks with your own vocabulary and kanji. Go to 'My Decks' in the navigation menu, click 'New Deck', and start adding content. You can also make your decks public to share with the community.

q1.question: How do I change my email or password?
q1.answer: Go to your profile settings and use the account management options. For password changes, use the 'Forgot Password' link on the login page. Email changes may require verification.

q2.question: How do I export my data?
q2.answer: You can export all your data (decks, progress, review history) from your account settings. The export includes everything in JSON format for your records.

q3.question: Can I delete my account?
q3.answer: Yes, you can delete your account at any time from your account settings. This will permanently remove all your data, including decks, progress, and study history. This action cannot be undone.

q1.question: I'm having trouble logging in.
q1.answer: Make sure you're using the correct email and password. If you've forgotten your password, use the 'Forgot Password' link. For OAuth login issues, try clearing your browser cookies or using a different browser.

q2.question: The app is not working properly.
q2.answer: Try refreshing the page or clearing your browser cache. If the issue persists, check if you're using a supported browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge). For persistent issues, contact support at support@watashiwa.app.

needMoreHelp.content: If you couldn't find what you're looking for, feel free to

About.metaTitle: About WatashiWa | The Cure for Burnout
About.metaDescription: WatashiWa: 3D learning for serious Japanese learners. Master vocabulary through the CUBE method—Context, Understanding, Blocking, Encoding. Free forever, open source.
About.title: About WatashiWa
About.subtitle: You are not dumb. Your method was flat (2D). Try 3D Learning.

section1.title: The Problem We Solve
section1.content: Most apps lose you at the 'Wall of Shame'—too many reviews due, too much rote memorization. WatashiWa intervenes in the forgetting process. We don't just show cards. We prime your brain, shield you from confusion, and encode memories that stick.

section2.title: The CUBE Method
section2.item1Desc: Never learn in isolation. Active priming stories connect new words to familiar concepts, targeting comprehensible input (90%+ known words).
section2.item2Desc: Etymology as the hook. Hán Việt roots reveal the logic behind Kanji—why 学 means 'study' connects to 学生 (student) and 学校 (school).
section2.item3Desc: Proactive interference shielding. When you confuse 箸 (chopsticks) with 橋 (bridge), the system triggers instant pitch-accent training to recalibrate your ear.
section2.item4Desc: Dynamic card variants adapt to your SRS stage. New cards show Kanji + audio. Learning cards test listening. Review cards demand context recall.
section2.item5Desc: Our intervention engine throttles new cards if reviews pile up. Less is more. Mastery achieved, not 'good job buddy.'

section4.title: Open Source, Free Forever
section4.content: WatashiWa is open source. The codebase lives on GitHub. We believe learning tools should be accessible, transparent, and community-driven.

section5.title: Built by a Learner, for Learners
section5.content: WatashiWa is a solo developer's response to the frustration of rote memorization. Born from the need to learn smarter, not harder.

section6.title: Our Promise
section6.item1: Free forever. No paywalls on core vocabulary.
section6.item2: Privacy first. Your data belongs to you. Export anytime.
section6.item3: Zen design. Calm, authoritative, no fluff. Mastery over gamification.

Profile.setupSubtitle: Set up your preferences to get started
Profile.cubeMethodTooltip: Our innovative method that groups related words together based on semantic connections, etymology, and context for deeper understanding.
Profile.srsMethodTooltip: Traditional spaced repetition system that prioritizes due reviews and new cards based on optimal timing.
Profile.cubeMethodSubtitle: Our innovative approach to Japanese vocabulary learning
Profile.cubeMethodIntroduction: CUBE (Context, Understanding, Blocking, Encoding) is WatashiWa's revolutionary learning method that goes beyond traditional spaced repetition. Instead of treating words as isolated items, CUBE creates meaningful connections between related vocabulary, helping you build a deeper understanding of Japanese.
Profile.cubeContextDesc: Words are presented with related vocabulary and contextual examples. Learn words in meaningful groups rather than random order.
Profile.cubeUnderstandingDesc: Deep etymology and Hán Việt connections help you understand the "why" behind words, not just memorize them.
Profile.cubeBlockingDesc: Our Confusion Shield proactively identifies and prevents mix-ups between similar words, homonyms, and pitch patterns.
Profile.cubeEncodingDesc: Dynamic practice variants adapt to your learning stage, ensuring words stick through active recall and varied contexts.
Profile.cubeBenefit1: Faster vocabulary acquisition through semantic connections
Profile.cubeBenefit2: Deeper understanding of word relationships and etymology
Profile.cubeBenefit3: Reduced confusion between similar words and homonyms
Profile.cubeBenefit4: More natural learning flow that mirrors how language is actually used
